headlines.headline: Latvian Defense Minister Resigns After Drone Airspace Violation
headlines.summary: Latvian Defense Minister Andris Spruds resigned after drones violated Latvian airspace, with PM Evika Silina saying he had lost her trust and that of the public.

headlines.headline: Trump May Relocate 5,000 US Troops to Poland From Germany
headlines.summary: President Trump said he may relocate 5,000 US troops to Poland, after previously pledging to withdraw forces from Germany following Chancellor Merz criticism of Washington Iran war handling.

headlines.headline: Turkey Pursues Intercontinental Ballistic Missile Capability
headlines.summary: An unexpected reveal of a previously unknown Turkish ICBM program has raised major questions about Ankara strategic ambitions and regional defense posture.

headlines.headline: Hantavirus Cruise Ship Passengers Evacuated in Tenerife
headlines.summary: About 150 passengers will be flown home from the MV Hondius cruise ship in Tenerife after a hantavirus outbreak, with UK and Ireland isolating affected passengers.

headlines.headline: South Korea Draws Gulf Oil Storage Interest as Hormuz Stays Closed
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia, Kuwait and the UAE are among major oil producers looking to South Korea underground storage facilities as the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Car Bomb Kills 15 Police Officers
headlines.summary: A suicide bomber attacked a police checkpoint in Pakistan late Saturday, killing 15 police officers. The attack highlights ongoing security challenges in the region.

headlines.headline: Russia Breaches Ukraine Ceasefire, Zelenskyy Says
headlines.summary: Russia violated a three-day ceasefire with Ukraine, according to President Zelenskyy, as Putin simultaneously hints at potential peace talks.

headlines.headline: British Paratroopers Deploy to Remote Island for Hantavirus Case
headlines.summary: Army parachutists landed on Tristan da Cunha, Britain's most remote overseas territory, to deliver medical aid for a suspected hantavirus case linked to the cruise ship outbreak.

headlines.headline: Gulf States Report Drone Attacks as Iran Ceasefire Strains
headlines.summary: A cargo ship was hit off Qatar's coast while Kuwait and the UAE repelled drone attacks, raising concerns about the stability of the US-Iran ceasefire.

headlines.headline: Trump Faces Diplomatic Tightrope Ahead of Xi Summit
headlines.summary: With Tehran, Taiwan, and trade tensions looming, the US president enters talks with China from a vulnerable position while hoping for economic wins.

headlines.headline: British paratroopers deploy to remote island for hantavirus case
headlines.summary: A specialist army team parachuted onto Tristan da Cunha, one of the world's most remote inhabited islands, to treat a Briton with suspected hantavirus from the affected cruise ship.

headlines.headline: Israel deports Gaza flotilla activists
headlines.summary: Spain's Saif Abu Keshek and Brazil's Thiago Ávila were deported by Israel after being detained aboard a Gaza-bound aid flotilla. The vessel was intercepted by Israeli authorities.

headlines.headline: Australia's One Nation wins historic parliamentary seat
headlines.summary: The right-wing One Nation party secured its first seat in the lower house in a by-election seen as a key test for the populist movement in Australian politics.

perspectives.angle: Australia's One Nation Party Wins Historic Parliamentary Seat
perspectives.summary: Pauline Hanson's right-wing One Nation party gained its first seat in Australia's lower house in a by-election seen as a key test for the populist party.

headlines.headline: UK Labour crisis deepens as Starmer faces leadership challenge
headlines.summary: Keir Starmer's position as UK Prime Minister appears increasingly precarious as around 40 Labour MPs call for his resignation. Health Secretary Wes Streeting and Deputy PM Angela Rayner are reportedly preparing leadership bids.

perspectives.angle: Starmer Faces Leadership Challenge as 40 Labour MPs Call for Resignation
perspectives.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is under mounting pressure with about 40 Labour MPs reportedly calling for him to quit as Angela Rayner and Wes Streeting eye leadership bids.

headlines.headline: DARPA seeks containerized drone swarm systems for autonomous operations
headlines.summary: DARPA issued a request for concepts for containerized systems that can launch, recover and support drone swarms of up to 500 UAVs, designed for deployment behind enemy lines with minimal human involvement.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Car Bomb Kills 15 Police Officers
headlines.summary: A suicide bomber rammed an explosives-laden vehicle into a checkpoint in Pakistan late Saturday night, killing at least 15 police officers in a devastating attack.

perspectives.angle: Pakistan car bomb attack kills 15 police officers
perspectives.summary: A suicide bomber rammed an explosives-laden vehicle into a checkpoint in Pakistan late Saturday night, killing 15 police officers in one of the deadliest attacks on security forces this year.

headlines.headline: Hungary's New PM Apologizes to Those Wronged Under Orbán
headlines.summary: Péter Magyar, who swept away 16 years of Viktor Orbán's rule, struck a radically different tone in his first speech as Hungary's new prime minister.

headlines.headline: Poland Finds Military Spy Drone Near Russian Kaliningrad Border
headlines.summary: Polish military police discovered a reconnaissance drone with Cyrillic markings near the border with Russia's Kaliningrad exclave. Officials say the drone had no combat capabilities but was likely used for intelligence gathering.

headlines.headline: Iran Sent Response to US War Proposal via Pakistan, State Media Reports
headlines.summary: Iran conveyed its response to a US proposal to end the conflict through Pakistani channels, according to Iranian state media. Pakistan has served as an intermediary in diplomatic communications between Washington and Tehran.

headlines.headline: US Sanctions Nine China and Hong Kong Entities Over Iran Military Links
headlines.summary: The US Treasury and State Department imposed sanctions on mainland China and Hong Kong entities accused of enabling Iran's military procurement efforts. The move comes days before Trump's summit with Xi Jinping in Beijing.

headlines.headline: Israel Allegedly Built Secret Military Outpost in Iraq for Iran Operations
headlines.summary: Reports indicate Israel established a clandestine military base in Iraq to support operations against Iran. The US reportedly advised Iraq to avoid the area where the facility was allegedly constructed.

headlines.headline: Iran responds to US ceasefire proposal as Gulf states report drone attacks
headlines.summary: Tehran has sent a response to Washington via mediator Pakistan regarding a 30-day ceasefire extension and Strait of Hormuz reopening, while drone strikes hit cargo ships off Qatar and were repelled by Kuwait and UAE.

headlines.headline: Putin says Ukraine conflict is coming to an end
headlines.summary: The Russian leader expressed optimism about potential Ukraine negotiations but criticized Western backing for President Zelensky, while aides cautioned it remains a long road to peace.

headlines.headline: Israel escalates Lebanon strikes, killing 39 despite ceasefire announcement
headlines.summary: Israeli attacks on Lebanon have killed at least 39 people, including an infant, as Israel and Hezbollah continue trading fire despite a ceasefire deal announced last month.

headlines.headline: DR Congo militia attack kills at least 69 civilians
headlines.summary: CODECO militia fighters killed at least 69 people in an attack in Ituri province, northeastern Democratic Republic of Congo, in one of the deadliest massacres in the region this year.

headlines.headline: WHO chief travels to Tenerife for hantavirus cruise ship evacuation
headlines.summary: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus arrived in Spain to oversee the evacuation of over 140 passengers and crew from a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship heading to the Canary Islands. Three people have died and five are infected in the outbreak.

perspectives.angle: Countries airlift nationals from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
perspectives.summary: Spanish passengers are the first to leave the MV Hondius after an outbreak that killed three people. Multiple nations are coordinating evacuations from the vessel docked in the Canary Islands.

headlines.headline: Trump feuds with allies likely to outlast Iran war, analysts warn
headlines.summary: Trump's words and deeds have revived fears among Washington's long-standing friends that the United States might be unreliable in a future crisis, with diplomatic tensions expected to persist even after the Iran conflict ends.

perspectives.angle: Marco Rubio Meets Pope Leo Amid Tensions With Trump Over Iran War
perspectives.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio held a meeting with Pope Leo, the first American pontiff, who has been a firm critic of the Iran war and the administration's immigration policies.

perspectives.angle: Pope Leo Appeals for Calm After Vatican Meeting With Rubio
perspectives.summary: Pope Leo asked God to inspire world leaders to reduce tensions and hatred in an address marking his first anniversary as head of the Catholic Church, following his meeting with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio amid strained U.S.-Holy See relations.

headlines.headline: Putin leads scaled-down Victory Day parade, vows victory in Ukraine
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin declared confidence in victory during Moscow's Victory Day parade, which for the first time in nearly two decades featured no tanks or heavy military equipment. Security was tight amid a US-brokered ceasefire.

headlines.headline: China unveils worlds first dual-core quantum computer
headlines.summary: Chinas Hanyuan-2 quantum computer operates at room temperature, marking a breakthrough in quantum computing accessibility and maintenance.

headlines.headline: Japans Mogami-class warship gaining ground in New Zealand defense competition
headlines.summary: Japans Mogami frigate and Britains Type 31 are top contenders for New Zealands naval modernization, with Australia potentially influencing the outcome.

perspectives.angle: Japan's Mogami-Class Warship Favored for New Zealand Frigate Competition
perspectives.summary: Japan's Mogami-class frigate has emerged as the leading contender for New Zealand's next-generation warship program, competing against Britain's Type 31 design, with Australia potentially influencing the outcome.

headlines.headline: Ukraine deploys AI-powered turrets against fiber-optic drones
headlines.summary: Ukrainian forces successfully deployed autonomous AI-driven turrets capable of intercepting Russian drones immune to electronic warfare.

headlines.headline: Iran accuses US of reckless military adventure amid Hormuz clashes
headlines.summary: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi accused the US of opting for military action over diplomacy as US forces disabled two Iranian tankers and fired on more Iranian vessels. Qatari PM urges negotiations as ceasefire holds despite ongoing exchanges.

headlines.headline: Hungary prepares for regime change as Magyar set to become PM
headlines.summary: Péter Magyar is being sworn in as Hungary new prime minister after his Tisza party won a landslide victory, ending 16 years of Viktor Orbán rule. The new government inherits a grim economic situation and promises asset recovery investigations.

headlines.headline: Bahrain Arrests 41 People Linked to Iran's IRGC
headlines.summary: Bahrain's interior ministry announced the arrest of 41 individuals it says were connected to Iran's Revolutionary Guards, amid heightened regional tensions following the US-Iran conflict.

perspectives.angle: Fragile US-Iran ceasefire holds as Bahrain arrests 41 linked to IRGC
perspectives.summary: A tenuous ceasefire appeared to be holding after US forces struck two Iranian oil tankers attempting to breach the American blockade. Bahrain arrested 41 people allegedly linked to Iran's Revolutionary Guard as Tehran weighs a US peace proposal.

perspectives.angle: US and Iran Exchange Fire as Fragile Ceasefire Shows Cracks
perspectives.summary: US military carried out retaliatory strikes on Iranian targets after renewed hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz. The UAE reported intercepting drones and missiles as Trump insisted the ceasefire still holds.

headlines.headline: Venice Biennale Torn by Controversy Over Russian Participation
headlines.summary: Russia's return to the Venice Biennale has sparked outrage, with Ukraine's culture minister comparing it to "inviting a serial killer to dinner with your friends" as the art world grapples with war's cultural impact.

headlines.headline: Zelenskiy confirms US-brokered three-day ceasefire and prisoner exchange with Russia
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy confirmed a three-day ceasefire arranged with Russia as part of US efforts to negotiate an end to the over four-year-old war.

perspectives.angle: Trump announces three-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire with prisoner swap
perspectives.summary: Donald Trump announced a three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, including a 1,000-for-1,000 prisoner exchange. Both Putin and Zelensky agreed to the truce after accusing each other of violations during Victory Day celebrations.

perspectives.angle: Trump Announces Russia-Ukraine 3-Day Ceasefire Agreement
perspectives.summary: President Trump said both Putin and Zelenskyy agreed to a three-day ceasefire and prisoner exchange, calling it a potential "beginning of the end" of the war.

perspectives.angle: Russia holds scaled-back Victory Day parade amid Ukraine war
perspectives.summary: Russia held its most scaled-back Victory Day parade in years due to threat of attack from Ukraine, where victory has proven elusive four years into the deadliest European conflict since WWII.

headlines.headline: Virginia Supreme Court throws out Democratic congressional map, boosts Republican midterm hopes
headlines.summary: The court rejected a Democratic-backed ballot measure that reconfigured the state's US House districts for partisan advantage, dealing a major blow to Democratic midterm prospects.

perspectives.angle: Virginia Supreme Court strikes down Democratic redistricting plan
perspectives.summary: Virginia highest court ruled 4-3 that Democrats violated procedural requirements when placing the congressional redistricting amendment on the ballot, delivering a setback to the party in the nationwide battle for House control ahead of midterms.

headlines.headline: Israeli artillery shells southern Lebanon, destroys homes in new strikes
headlines.summary: Israeli forces continue targeting towns in southern Lebanon despite ceasefire arrangements, with artillery strikes hitting Braashit, Safad al-Battikh, and Touline amid ongoing truce violations.

perspectives.angle: Hezbollah Claims 13 Attacks on Israeli Forces in Southern Lebanon
perspectives.summary: Hezbollah announced it carried out drone and artillery strikes on Israeli troops, Merkava tanks, an Iron Dome platform, and military positions in southern Lebanon amid ongoing cross-border escalation.

headlines.headline: Erdogan says Europe without Türkiye is incomplete and vulnerable in crisis management
headlines.summary: Marking Europe Day, President Erdogan emphasized Turkiye as an essential partner for European security and crisis response, calling for a more inclusive EU amid global challenges.

headlines.headline: France seeks Africa reset at Kenya summit after West Africa setbacks
headlines.summary: Paris aims to rebuild influence in Africa following losses in former colonies, co-hosting a regional summit in Kenya as part of a strategic pivot away from its troubled West African relationships.

headlines.headline: Ukraine running critically low on air defence missiles after winter campaign
headlines.summary: Ukraine's air force warned it is running short of air defence missiles following Russia's massive winter attack campaign, as Kyiv braces for further strikes amid ongoing conflict.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine running short of air defence missiles after Russian winter campaign
perspectives.summary: Ukraine's air force warned it is running short of air defence missiles after Russia's massive winter attack campaign, as it braces for further strikes.

headlines.headline: US trade chief says Trump administration will win appeal on temporary tariffs ruling
headlines.summary: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said the administration expects to prevail in appealing the Court of International Trade's ruling against its temporary 10% global tariffs.

perspectives.angle: Trump Administration Appeals Court Ruling Against Global Tariffs
perspectives.summary: The administration challenged a trade court decision that struck down the 10% global tariff imposed in February, with the US Trade Representative expressing confidence in winning the appeal.

headlines.headline: US and Iran Remain Deadlocked Despite Ongoing Gulf Clashes
headlines.summary: Washington awaits Tehran's response to a US proposal to formally end the war, but neither side appears closer to resolution as military confrontations continue in the Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.angle: CIA assesses Iran could withstand naval blockade for four months as Gulf clashes continue
perspectives.summary: A US intelligence analysis indicates Iran has stockpiles and resilience to endure a naval blockade for months, suggesting the Islamic Republic has not been forced to capitulate despite the US-led campaign in the Gulf.

perspectives.angle: CIA Report: Iran Could Withstand Hormuz Blockade for Four Months
perspectives.summary: A US intelligence analysis concludes Tehran has stockpiles to survive a naval blockade for months, as fighting flares in the Gulf despite ongoing ceasefire negotiations.

perspectives.angle: Iran accuses US of reckless military adventure as Hormuz clashes continue
perspectives.summary: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi accused the US of opting for military action whenever diplomacy is on the table, as both sides exchanged attacks in the Strait of Hormuz and the US enforced a naval blockade of Iranian ports.

headlines.headline: US Warns EU of Higher Tariffs if Trade Deal Misses July 4 Deadline
headlines.summary: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer stated that the US will revert to higher tariffs on European goods if Brussels fails to implement trade commitments by the July 4 deadline.

perspectives.angle: Trump Gives EU Until July 4 to Ratify Trade Deal or Face Higher Tariffs
perspectives.summary: President Trump set a two-month deadline for the European Union to implement the trade deal agreed last year, threatening significantly higher tariffs if not fulfilled by Independence Day.

headlines.headline: India appoints new Chief of Defence Staff amid major military reforms
headlines.summary: Retired Lt. Gen. NS Raja Subramani named India's next Chief of Defence Staff, succeeding Anil Chauhan as the country undertakes its biggest military overhaul to modernize and integrate the army, navy, and air force.

headlines.headline: Plaid Cymru wins most seats in Wales Senedd election, ready to form government
headlines.summary: Rhun ap Iorwerth's Plaid Cymru won the most seats in the Welsh Parliament election, signalling a shift in Welsh politics as the party prepares to form a new government after a historic victory.

headlines.headline: Russian casualties in Ukraine reach 1.34 million, says Kyiv
headlines.summary: Ukraine's General Staff reports Russia has lost approximately 1,340,270 troops since the full-scale invasion began in February 2022, in one of the highest casualty tolls of any modern conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump appeals court loss on tariffs as trade policy chaos deepens
headlines.summary: The Trump administration appealed a court ruling that its 10% global tariff imposed in February was not justified, adding to uncertainty in global trade as tensions with China and other partners continue.

headlines.headline: US Suspects Billions in Nvidia Chips Smuggled to China via Thailand
headlines.summary: A firm linked to Thailand national AI initiative is suspected of helping smuggle Super Micro servers containing advanced Nvidia chips to China, Bloomberg reported. The alleged sanctions evasion highlights ongoing chip export control challenges.

perspectives.angle: US suspects Nvidia chips smuggled to China via Thailand in billions-dollar scheme
perspectives.summary: A firm linked to Thailand national AI initiative is suspected of helping smuggle Super Micro servers with advanced Nvidia chips to China, Bloomberg reported, amid ongoing US-China tech tensions.

headlines.headline: Norway Holds Chinese Woman on Suspicion of Espionage
headlines.summary: A Chinese woman will be held in police custody for up to four weeks while investigators probe suspected espionage activities in Norway, a court ruled Friday.

perspectives.angle: Norway holds Chinese woman in custody on suspected espionage charges
perspectives.summary: A Norwegian court ruled that a Chinese woman suspected of complicity in espionage will be held in police custody for up to four weeks while authorities investigate the case.

headlines.headline: Greece Investigates Ukrainian Naval Drone Found on Island
headlines.summary: Greek authorities are investigating a drone boat discovered by fishermen in a cave on the Ionian island of Lefkada. The drone was identified as Ukrainian-made, raising questions about its presence in Greek waters.

perspectives.angle: Greek authorities investigate Ukrainian-made naval drone found in island cave
perspectives.summary: Police and coast guard sources are investigating a drone boat discovered by fishermen in a cave on the Ionian island of Lefkada, raising questions about its origin and purpose.

headlines.headline: Venice Biennale Jury Resigns Over Israel and Russia Participation
headlines.summary: Geopolitical tensions have spilled into the Venice Biennale contemporary art exhibition, with the jury resigning in protest over Israeli and Russian participation in this years edition.

headlines.headline: Belarus Expands Military Infrastructure With Russian Coordination
headlines.summary: Ukrainian border officials report Belarus is developing training grounds and logistics routes that could support Russian operations against Ukraine, though no immediate offensive threat exists.

headlines.headline: Poland Signs Record €44 Billion EU Defense Loan
headlines.summary: Poland becomes the largest beneficiary of the EU SAFE program, securing preferential loans for defense modernization as Europe accelerates rearmament amid Russian threat and concerns over US commitment.

headlines.headline: Russia Still Outproducing EU Militarily, Defence Commissioner Warns
headlines.summary: EU Defence Commissioner Andrius Kubilius says Russia produces more ammunition in three months than NATO produces in a year, urging European defense industry to scale up faster.

headlines.headline: Global Cyberattack Disrupts Thousands of Schools and Universities Worldwide
headlines.summary: Hacking group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for a major ransomware attack on Canvas, the academic software platform used by approximately 9,000 educational institutions globally. Students across the US, Canada, Australia and Europe faced exam disruptions as ransom demands appeared on screens during final exam season.

perspectives.angle: Major cyber attack disrupts thousands of universities and schools worldwide
perspectives.summary: Hacking group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for an attack on Canvas academic software, affecting approximately 9,000 institutions globally. Students faced exam cancellations and coursework disruptions across US, Canada, Australia and Europe.

headlines.headline: Hungary Swears in New Prime Minister After Viktor Orban Era Ends
headlines.summary: Peter Magyar of the Tisza party is being sworn in as Hungary's new prime minister after a landslide victory ended 16 years of Viktor Orban's rule. The new government faces economic challenges and must negotiate the release of €17 billion in frozen EU funds.

headlines.headline: South African Opposition Calls for Ramaphosa Resignation After Court Ruling
headlines.summary: South Africa's Constitutional Court ruled that parliament violated the constitution by blocking impeachment proceedings against President Cyril Ramaphosa over the Farmgate scandal involving stolen cash at his farm. Opposition leader Julius Malema called for Ramaphosa to resign.

perspectives.angle: South African Opposition Demands Ramaphosa Resign After Court Ruling
perspectives.summary: Opposition leader Julius Malema called for President Cyril Ramaphosa to resign after the Constitutional Court ruled parliament violated the constitution by blocking impeachment proceedings over the Phala Phala cash theft scandal.

headlines.headline: Mystery Naval Drone With Explosives Found in Greek Waters
headlines.summary: Greek military is examining an unmanned naval drone discovered by fishermen near the island of Lefkada. Reports suggest the vessel may be Ukrainian-made and could be linked to operations against Russia's "shadow fleet" or the broader Ukraine-Russia conflict.

headlines.headline: Jimmy Lai's Fate May Turn on Trump-Xi Meeting Next Week
headlines.summary: The imprisoned Hong Kong pro-democracy activist's family hopes Trump will seek his release during an upcoming Beijing summit. The 78-year-old media mogul was sentenced to 20 years under China's national security law, and observers say his release could signal whether Beijing is ready to move on.

headlines.headline: Africa's EV Surge Led by Ethiopia as Fuel Shortages Mount
headlines.summary: Africa imported 44,358 electric vehicles from China in 2025, up from 19,386 the prior year, with Ethiopia leading adoption after banning new fossil fuel vehicle imports. The Iran war's disruption of oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz has accelerated the transition away from costly fuel imports.

headlines.headline: Israel Wounds 9 Palestinians Including Child in Gaza Ceasefire Violation
headlines.summary: Gaza Civil Defense reports that Israeli forces wounded 9 Palestinians, including a child, in the latest violation of the ceasefire agreement.

headlines.headline: US to Host Israel-Lebanon Talks Pressing for Hezbollah Disarmament
headlines.summary: Washington will host new Israel-Lebanon negotiations as the Trump team pushes for Hezbollah disarmament, while awaiting Iran's response to a US proposal to end regional conflict.

perspectives.angle: US to Host New Israel-Lebanon Talks on Hezbollah Disarmament
perspectives.summary: The Trump administration presses for Hezbollah disarmament as it awaits Iran response to a new US proposal aimed at advancing peace in the region.

headlines.headline: S&P Maintains Israel Credit Rating at A Despite Iran War
headlines.summary: S&P Global Ratings affirmed Israel's A/A-1 credit rating with stable outlook, citing resilience despite ongoing Iran conflict and regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Rubio Questions Allied Support on Iran After Italy Talks
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and questioned why allies including Italy were not backing U.S. efforts to confront Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.angle: Rubio Questions Allies Support on Iran After Italy Talks
perspectives.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Italian PM Giorgia Meloni and questioned why allies including Italy were not backing Washington efforts to confront Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting strains in the US-Europe relationship over the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: UK Markets Understate Political Dysfunction After Reform Surge
headlines.summary: British bond yields remained calm after Reform UK gains in local elections, but the rise of fringe parties bodes ill for economic policy coherence, with upstarts less likely than current government to deliver coherent policy.

headlines.headline: India Inflation Likely Rose to 3.8% as Fuel Costs Climb
headlines.summary: India annual consumer inflation likely moved closer to the central bank 4% target in April as higher fuel costs from the U.S.-Iran conflict began feeding into prices, according to a Reuters poll of economists.

headlines.headline: US concerned Taiwan defense delays are "concession" to China
headlines.summary: The US State Department warned that further delays to Taiwan's military spending represent a 'concession' to China, as Taipei's defense ministry detailed the impact of projects excluded from a budget package.

headlines.headline: Paraguay president affirms strong Taiwan ties during Taipei visit
headlines.summary: Paraguay President Santiago Pena said his country "deeply values" relations with Taiwan and will continue to offer support, during a formal welcome in Taipei.

perspectives.angle: Paraguay reaffirms Taiwan ties as China pressures last South American ally
perspectives.summary: Paraguay President Santiago Pena visited Taipei to reaffirm diplomatic support for Taiwan, one of only 12 countries maintaining formal relations. China has intensified efforts to draw Paraguay away, with lawmakers visiting Beijing amid internal debate over economic costs.

headlines.headline: British MPs to visit China for first time in seven years
headlines.summary: A cross-party delegation of British lawmakers will visit China this month in a sign of warming ties after Beijing agreed to lift sanctions on six MPs following Prime Minister Keir Starmer's January visit.

perspectives.angle: British MPs to Visit China This Month for First Time in Seven Years
perspectives.summary: A cross-party delegation of British lawmakers will visit China this month, in a sign of warming ties after Beijing agreed to lift sanctions on six MPs following Prime Minister Keir Starmers January visit.

headlines.headline: France arrests Ukrainian linked to notorious Russian torture prison in occupied Donetsk
headlines.summary: French authorities detained a suspect accused of assisting the head of the Izolyatsia prison camp in torturing inmates, marking the second time a foreign country has detained a suspect charged with war crimes in Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Massive forest fire sparked by Russian strikes in Ukraine doubles in size to 4,300 hectares
headlines.summary: The forest fire near Ukraine's border with Russia has expanded from 2,400 to 4,300 hectares (10,600 acres), with FPV drones reportedly blocking firefighter access to the area.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine strikes Russian oil refinery in Perm region for third time in two weeks
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian drones attacked oil infrastructure in Russia's Perm region overnight, marking the third strike on the facility in recent weeks amid continued attacks on Russian energy infrastructure.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Refinery in Perm for Third Time
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian forces hit the Lukoil-Permnefteorgsintez refinery in Perm, marking the third strike on the facility in two weeks. The attacks are part of Ukraine's campaign against Russian energy infrastructure.

headlines.headline: EU prepares to back Belgium in frozen assets fight against Russian oligarchs
headlines.summary: Russian oligarchs are using a 1989 Soviet-era investment treaty to challenge frozen asset seizures, prompting Belgium to seek EU support in the legal battle over sanctions enforcement.

headlines.headline: ASEAN leaders agree to create shared fuel reserve for future crises
headlines.summary: Southeast Asian nations are establishing a regional fuel stockpile to strengthen energy security and resilience against supply disruptions, amid growing concerns about global instability.

headlines.headline: US Sanctions Chinese Firms for Aiding Iran Drone Program
headlines.summary: The US Treasury sanctioned 10 individuals and companies from China and Hong Kong for supplying materials for Iran's Shahed drones and ballistic missiles, escalating pressure on Tehran's weapons supply chain.

headlines.headline: US Revises UN Iran Resolution, China and Russia Still Expected to Veto
headlines.summary: Washington revised its proposed UN resolution demanding Iran halt attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, but diplomats say the changes are unlikely to prevent Chinese and Russian vetoes.

perspectives.angle: US Revises UN Resolution on Iran But China Russia Still Expected to Veto
perspectives.summary: Washington has revised its proposed UN resolution demanding Iran halt attacks and mining in the Strait of Hormuz, but the changes are unlikely to avert Chinese and Russian vetoes, diplomats said.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Approves $25 Billion Defense Boost, Less Than Government Sought
headlines.summary: Taiwan's opposition-controlled parliament approved extra defense spending of $25 billion, about two-thirds of what the government requested to counter China's military modernization.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan Parliament Approves Extra Defense Spending
perspectives.summary: Taiwans opposition-controlled parliament approved extra defense spending of $25 billion, about two-thirds of what the government sought, to bolster armed forces facing a rapidly modernizing Chinese military.

headlines.headline: Pro-Independence Parties Surge Across Britain in Historic Election Shift
headlines.summary: Three of the United Kingdom's four nations are now set to be governed by pro-independence parties after elections that nationalists say mark a turning point for the centuries-old union.

headlines.headline: US Strikes Two Iranian-Flagged Oil Tankers in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: US Central Command confirmed strikes on two empty Iranian oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz as tensions escalate despite ongoing ceasefire negotiations. The incident marks another challenge to the fragile truce.

perspectives.angle: US strikes Iranian tankers as Gulf tensions threaten ceasefire
perspectives.summary: US forces struck tankers attempting to break Iran blockade in the Gulf of Oman; Iran questioned American diplomacy seriousness amid renewed clashes.

perspectives.angle: US strikes Iranian ships in Gulf of Oman
perspectives.summary: US Central Command released video showing F/A-18 Super Hornets using precision munitions against two Iranian tankers as fighting continues around the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Ex-NATO Chief Warns of Alliance Disintegration
headlines.summary: Former NATO Secretary General Anders Fogh Rasmussen warned of potential alliance disintegration as European countries increasingly take leadership roles while the US role recedes under Trump administration policies.

headlines.headline: Foreign Militias Deployed in Iran to Enforce Regime Crackdown
headlines.summary: Iraqi Hashd al-Shaabi militias are now patrolling Iranian streets as the regime intensifies its internal crackdown, with residents saying it feels like an occupation.

headlines.headline: Iran Questions US Commitment to Diplomacy After Naval Escalation
headlines.summary: Foreign Minister Araghchi told his Turkish counterpart that US naval escalation in the Strait of Hormuz raises doubts about Washingtons commitment to ongoing peace talks.

headlines.headline: Four Convicted in US Trial Over Haiti Presidential Assassination
headlines.summary: A US jury convicted four people of conspiracy in connection with the 2021 assassination of Haitian President Jovenel Moïse, with prosecutors saying the conspirators hoped to enrich themselves.

headlines.headline: Japan Sees Largest Anti-War Protests in Decades Over Defense Expansion
headlines.summary: Thousands of protesters gathered in Tokyo and other cities against Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi moves to lift arms export restrictions and expand military role abroad, challenging Japan post-war pacifist constitution.

headlines.headline: Iran says US cannot turn China against Tehran as peace talks loom
headlines.summary: Iran ambassador to China says Beijing-Tehran ties are strong enough to withstand US pressure, as speculation grows Washington may offer trade concessions to China in exchange for pressuring Iran on peace deal.

perspectives.angle: US-Iran peace talks may resume next week in Islamabad
perspectives.summary: Wall Street Journal reports the US and Iran could resume negotiations as early as next week in Pakistans capital, citing people familiar with the matter, as both sides seek to end the conflict.

headlines.headline: Two LNG tankers cross Hormuz en route to Japan and China
headlines.summary: Two liquefied natural gas carriers have passed through the Strait of Hormuz heading to Japan and China, signaling possible easing of the blockade, though volumes remain small and Asian spot prices are up 60%.

headlines.headline: European Shares Slide as Middle East Tensions Flare
headlines.summary: European shares slipped on Friday in broad-based losses as a flare-up in the Middle East conflict hit risk sentiment at the end of a week dominated by geopolitics.

headlines.headline: Starmer Vows to Stay On Despite Labour Election Disaster
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer insisted he would not resign after disastrous local elections saw Labour lose thousands of council seats and control of major cities including Birmingham, while Reform UK made historic gains.

perspectives.angle: Starmer vows to stay on after Labour suffers local election drubbing
perspectives.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer rejected calls to resign after local elections delivered heavy losses for the Labour Party, insisting he will continue leading despite the poor results.

headlines.headline: UK Elections: Reform UK Surges as Labour Suffers Major Losses
headlines.summary: Nigel Farage's hard-right Reform UK party made significant gains in England's local elections while Keir Starmer's Labour Party suffered major losses, with pro-independence parties now governing three of the UK's four nations.

perspectives.angle: UK Labour Party Suffers Heavy Losses as Reform UK Surges
perspectives.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer vowed to fight on after his Labour Party suffered heavy losses in local elections, with Nigel Farage's Reform UK making significant gains that deepened doubts over Labour's ability to govern.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo XIV Marks First Year Amid Tensions With Trump
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV sought to focus on a pastoral role in his first year as pontiff, but found himself in verbal sparring matches with Trump over global tensions and Vatican policy.

headlines.headline: Beijing Condemns UK Conviction of Two Men Tied to Hong Kong Trade Office
headlines.summary: China's foreign ministry accused the UK of abusing the law after Bill Yuen and Peter Wai were found guilty of spying on Hong Kong activists in Britain.

headlines.headline: Iraq's Sadr Calls to Exclude Armed Groups From Next Government
headlines.summary: Shia cleric Muqtada al-Sadr urges exclusion of armed groups from Iraq's next government as Zaidi attempts to form a cabinet amid US pressure to curb Iran-backed actors.

headlines.headline: China exports accelerate in April as Iran war drives global stockpiling
headlines.summary: Chinese export growth likely quickened in April as companies worldwide rushed to secure components amid fears the Iran conflict could push input costs higher, a Reuters poll shows.

perspectives.angle: China Exports Surge as Companies Stockpile Amid Iran War Fears
perspectives.summary: Chinese export growth quickened in April as firms rushed to stockpile components, worried the Iran conflict could push input costs higher.

perspectives.angle: Gold Fields flags cost pressures as Iran war drives up input prices
perspectives.summary: South Africas Gold Fields expects costs to increase as the Iran war has driven prices of fuel and explosives higher, highlighting conflict impact on mining sector.

headlines.headline: German industrial production unexpectedly falls 0.7% in March
headlines.summary: German industrial output dropped unexpectedly in March, decreasing 0.7% from the previous month, the federal statistics office reported, signaling continued weakness in Europe largest economy.

headlines.headline: US Expects Iranian Response to Peace Proposal as Fighting Flares in Gulf
headlines.summary: The United States expects an Iranian response as soon as Friday to its latest proposal to end the war, even as US and Iranian forces traded fire and the UAE came under renewed attack.

perspectives.angle: US expects Iran response to peace proposal tonight
perspectives.summary: President Trump said he expects Iran response to a US peace proposal soon, while US and Iran continue exchanging fire in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Dollar Slips as Investors Pin Hopes on US-Iran Peace Resolution
headlines.summary: The dollar slipped Friday, on pace for a second straight weekly fall as investors stayed cautiously optimistic about a swift end to the Middle East conflict after Trump said the ceasefire remained in place.

perspectives.angle: Gold Rises for Third Day on Hopes for US-Iran Peace Agreement
perspectives.summary: Gold prices extended gains as a softer dollar and optimism about diplomatic efforts to end the Middle East conflict supported the precious metal.

headlines.headline: Questions Mount Over UK PM Starmer Future After Election Losses
headlines.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has vowed to carry on as leader after his Labour Party suffered heavy losses to the populist Reform party, deepening doubts over his ability to govern.

headlines.headline: Kremlin Says Putin Ready to Negotiate With EU But Wont Make First Move
headlines.summary: President Vladimir Putin is ready to negotiate with everyone including the Europeans, the Kremlin said, after reports EU leaders were preparing for potential talks.

headlines.headline: Global Perceptions of US Fall Below Russia Under Trump, Survey Finds
headlines.summary: Global perceptions of the US have deteriorated for a second consecutive year and are now worse than views of Russia, an annual study showed, as Trumps policies continue to strain the NATO alliance.

headlines.headline: Turkey-Iran Foreign Ministers Discuss Status of US-Iran Talks
headlines.summary: Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan held a phone call with Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi to review the status of negotiations between Washington and Tehran amid fragile ceasefire.

headlines.headline: US Opposes Israeli West Bank Annexation, Diplomat Tells UN
headlines.summary: US ambassador to UN confirms Washington opposes annexation plans, saying regular dialogue continues with partners about West Bank stability and security.

headlines.headline: Rubio Backs NATO in Rome Amid Rising US-Italy Tensions
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met PM Giorgia Meloni in Rome, reaffirming NATO support amid simmering tensions between Washington and Rome in recent weeks.

perspectives.angle: European NATO members step up as US role in alliance recedes amid Iran war tensions
perspectives.summary: Trump decision to strike Iran without NATO consultation has inflamed tensions with European allies. US announces 5,000 troop withdrawal from Germany, while Spain refused access to joint bases and Britain distanced itself from US Iran policy.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Releases First Batch of Previously Classified UFO Files
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth released decades of previously classified UFO sightings recorded by FBI, NASA and other federal agencies in a major transparency push.

headlines.headline: Seven Markets to Track If Peace Breaks Out in Middle East
headlines.summary: A durable peace breakthrough in the Middle East would ripple through markets far beyond crude oil, quickly stripping out risk premia baked into freight rates, fertilizer prices and more.

headlines.headline: Iran Seizes Oil Tanker as US Disables Two Ships in Gulf
headlines.summary: Iran said it seized an oil tanker over attempts to disrupt its exports, hours after US and Iranian forces exchanged fire in the Strait of Hormuz, with US forces firing on and disabling two Iranian tankers.

headlines.headline: IEA Warns Energy Markets Headed Into Troubled Waters
headlines.summary: Global energy markets are headed into "troubled waters" as the Iran war continues to knock out millions of barrels of oil each day, IEA head Fatih Birol warned, saying Europe made a mistake moving away from nuclear power.

headlines.headline: Toyota Warns of $4.3 Billion Hit from Iran War
headlines.summary: Japan's Toyota expects the Iran conflict to cost it about $4.3 billion this financial year, one of the most significant corporate warnings yet on the wider economic impact of the US-Israeli war on Iran.

perspectives.angle: $7 Billion in Well-Timed Oil Bets Before Iran War News Sparks Scrutiny
perspectives.summary: Legal experts and lawmakers are calling for investigations into whether well-timed oil trades before the Iran war news were based on inside information or leaks.

perspectives.angle: Toyota Forecasts 20% Profit Drop as Iran War Disrupts Supply Chains
perspectives.summary: Toyota halved its quarterly profit and forecast a 20% annual decline as supply chain disruptions from the Iran war and rising costs weigh on the world's largest automaker.

headlines.headline: US Trade Court Rules Trump Tariffs Illegal, Blocks 10% Global Levy
headlines.summary: A federal trade court ruled that President Trump exceeded his authority with new 10% global tariffs, though the order only applies to importers who sued. The administration plans to appeal.

perspectives.angle: US trade court rules Trump 10% global tariff illegal
perspectives.summary: A panel of federal judges on the Court of International Trade blocked President Trump's 10% tariffs on most US imports, ruling in a 2-1 decision that the law sidestepped a Supreme Court ruling that struck down blanket tariffs imposed under emergency powers.

headlines.headline: US Navy Came Under Iranian Missile Fire After Attacking Oil Tanker
headlines.summary: Iranian state media reports US naval forces in the Strait of Hormuz came under missile fire following a US attack on an Iranian oil tanker, marking a significant escalation in the Gulf conflict.

perspectives.angle: US fires on Iranian oil tanker amid war pressure
perspectives.summary: US military forces fired on an Iranian oil tanker as the Trump administration continues to pressure Tehran for a deal to end the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: North Korea Deploys Artillery at Border to Hit Seoul
headlines.summary: Kim Jong Un inspected production of artillery with 60km+ range to be deployed at the South Korean border, bolstering its ability to strike Seoul and other targets.

perspectives.angle: North Korea Deploys New Artillery Systems Capable of Striking Seoul
perspectives.summary: Kim Jong Un inspected new long-range artillery with ranges exceeding 60km to be deployed at the South Korean border, bolstering ability to hit Seoul, state media reports.

headlines.headline: China Fireworks Factory Blast Kills 37, One Missing
headlines.summary: The death toll from a fireworks factory explosion in Hunan province rose to 37 with one person still missing, making it China's deadliest industrial blast since 2019.

headlines.headline: US Marine Corps Seeks Robots to Build Expeditionary Airfields
headlines.summary: The Marine Corps is developing robotic systems to construct forward airfields, part of its Force Design 2030 modernization. The initiative aims to reduce personnel needs and speed up deployment in contested environments.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Deploys 25,000 Ground Robots for Front-Line Combat
headlines.summary: Ukraine is producing 25,000 unmanned ground vehicles to serve as "cavalry" on the front lines, reducing casualties and replacing lost hardware. The robots represent a major shift in modern warfare tactics.

headlines.headline: NPR: UAE intercepts missiles as US-Iran fighting flares
headlines.summary: The UAE engaged air defenses against Iranian missiles and drones as the US-Iran ceasefire frayed. Washington expects Irans response to its peace proposal later on Friday.

headlines.headline: Rubio admits US peace mediation in Ukraine has stalled
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio acknowledged that US efforts to broker peace in Ukraine have not led to a fruitful outcome so far, marking a frank admission of the diplomatic deadlock.

headlines.headline: Trump Announces Withdrawal of 5,000 US Troops from Germany
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump announced he will withdraw 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany as a rift over the Iran war widens between Washington and NATO allies, raising questions about the legal and practical implications of such a move.

perspectives.angle: Trump Announces Withdrawal of 5,000 US Troops from Germany
perspectives.summary: President Trump said he will withdraw 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany as tensions with NATO allies widen over the Iran war, raising questions about the future of the transatlantic alliance.

headlines.headline: Dollar Heads for Second Weekly Fall Despite US-Iran Clashes
headlines.summary: The dollar was set for its second straight weekly decline as investors remained cautiously optimistic about a diplomatic resolution to the Iran conflict, even after renewed hostilities near the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: David Attenborough Turns 100, "Overwhelmed" by Global Tributes
headlines.summary: The legendary British wildlife broadcaster David Attenborough turned 100 on Friday, receiving worldwide accolades for his decades of work bringing nature documentaries to millions. Attenborough said he was "completely overwhelmed" by the outpouring of birthday wishes.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Could Sign Gripen Fighter Deal Within Months, Sweden Says
headlines.summary: Ukraine's purchase of Swedish Saab Gripen fighter jets could be signed within months, Sweden's defense minister said during a visit to Stockholm. The deal would bolster Ukraine's air capabilities as the war with Russia continues.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine-Sweden Gripen Fighter Deal Could Be Signed Within Months
perspectives.summary: Ukraine's defense minister says talks for Saab-made Gripen fighter jets are progressing well, with a potential deal signed within months during his visit to Stockholm.

headlines.headline: Nigeria begins court martial of 36 officers over alleged coup plot
headlines.summary: Nigeria's government faces mounting pressure as 36 senior military officers stand trial before a special court martial over an alleged 2025 coup plot, raising concerns about the country's worsening security situation.

headlines.headline: Turkish defense firms secure $8 billion in export deals at SAHA Expo
headlines.summary: Turkish defense companies signed nearly $8 billion in export contracts during the first three days of SAHA Expo, a major defense trade show, highlighting Turkey's growing role as a defense exporter.

perspectives.angle: Turkish Defense Industry Showcases New Systems at SAHA 2026
perspectives.summary: Turkish defense manufacturers presented new-generation systems at SAHA 2026 in Istanbul, highlighting Türkiye indigenous military production capabilities.

headlines.headline: Bahrain expels three MPs after they voted against royal citizenship decree
headlines.summary: Bahrain has revoked the parliamentary membership of three lawmakers just days after dozens had their nationalities stripped in connection with the Iran war context, amid a widening crackdown on dissent.

headlines.headline: Iran seizes oil tanker Ocean Koi in Gulf of Oman
headlines.summary: Iran's navy seized an oil tanker named Ocean Koi in the Gulf of Oman over an alleged attempt to disrupt Iran's oil exports, Iranian state media reported, adding to escalating tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: WHO documents over 3,000 attacks on Ukraine healthcare since war began
headlines.summary: The World Health Organization has registered more than 3,000 attacks on Ukraine's healthcare system since the start of the war, marking one of the highest numbers of attacks on healthcare in any conflict.

headlines.headline: UAE intercepts drone and missile attack as Iran war ceasefire frays
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates said it intercepted missiles and drones as tensions escalate despite ongoing ceasefire negotiations between Iran and the US. The UAE Ministry of Defense said its air defenses were "actively engaging" incoming threats.

perspectives.angle: UAE responds to Iranian missile and drone attacks as ceasefire frays
perspectives.summary: The United Arab Emirates said it was responding to Iranian attacks hours after the US and Iran traded fire in the Strait of Hormuz. Around 1,500 ships remain trapped in the Gulf due to the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Secret document reveals Russia's plans to supply advanced weapons to Iran
headlines.summary: A classified document obtained by The Economist shows Russia offered to provide Iran with innovative weapons in large enough quantities to inflict significant casualties on American and allied forces, beyond the intelligence support Moscow is already believed to have provided.

headlines.headline: Indonesia volcano eruption kills three hikers on remote island
headlines.summary: Mount Dukono erupted on Halmahera Island, killing three hikers including two Singaporeans. Rescuers evacuated 14 climbers while searching for others who defied safety restrictions.

headlines.headline: Islamic State-affiliated group kills 40 in Congo near Uganda border
headlines.summary: Allied Democratic Forces attacked villages in eastern Congo overnight, killing at least 40 and burning homes. The group pledged allegiance to IS in 2019 and has long operated in the border region.

headlines.headline: Lebanon civil defense member killed in Israeli strike amid ceasefire
headlines.summary: An Israeli strike in southern Lebanon killed a civil defense rescuer despite the ongoing truce. WHO has verified 152 attacks on healthcare in Lebanon since the war began, with 103 deaths.

headlines.headline: China sees weakened US deterrence on Taiwan as Iran war drains weapons
headlines.summary: Chinese analysts say America"s ability to deter a war over Taiwan is weakened by the Iran conflict, giving Beijing leverage ahead of President Trump"s upcoming summit with Xi Jinping.

perspectives.angle: China Expands Influence Across Asia as Iran War Creates Energy Crisis
perspectives.summary: The Iran war has left China's neighbors appealing for energy help, handing Beijing the regional sway it has long sought. Countries across Asia are turning to China as fuel shortages bite.

headlines.headline: UAE sends $100 million to train new Gaza police force under Trump-backed plan
headlines.summary: The UAE has transferred $100 million to Trump's Board of Peace to fund training of a 27,000-strong Gaza police force composed of Jordanian and Egyptian-trained officers, marking a significant step in post-war governance planning.

headlines.headline: China's J-35 stealth fighters to give Pakistan edge over India in fifth-generation tech
headlines.summary: Pakistan is set to acquire Chinese J-35 stealth jets, potentially giving it a technological advantage over India which lacks fifth-generation fighters despite having a larger overall fleet.

headlines.headline: German cyber chief warns: 'We are not at war, but we're not at peace either'
headlines.summary: Germany's cyber warfare commander says allied nations face relentless digital attacks from Russia and other adversaries, with cyber intrusions increasingly targeting defense industries as European rearmament accelerates.

headlines.headline: US-Philippine Balikatan drills expand with more nations and advanced missiles
headlines.summary: American and Filipino forces wrapped up annual exercises featuring troops from five additional nations and deployed advanced systems including NMESIS and HIMARS, demonstrating deepening Indo-Pacific security cooperation.

headlines.headline: IOC Lifts All Restrictions on Belarusian Athletes
headlines.summary: The International Olympic Committee removed all restrictions on Belarusian athletes, clearing them for international competitions including LA 2028 Olympic qualifiers.

headlines.headline: Vance and Rubio Take Center Stage as 2028 Republican Contenders
headlines.summary: The Vice President and Secretary of State offered contrasting styles this week, signaling early positioning for a potential post-Trump Republican future.

headlines.headline: ASEAN Leaders Meet on Iran War Economic Fallout
headlines.summary: Southeast Asian leaders are meeting under pressure to mitigate the impact on their economies from the Iran war, with one minister saying it "should not have occurred in the first place."

headlines.headline: Pakistan Seeks Singapore's Help for Return of Seafarers From US-Seized Vessels
headlines.summary: Pakistan's foreign minister requested Singapore's assistance in repatriating Pakistani and Iranian seafarers aboard vessels seized by the US amid the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Israel Opposition Bloc Could Secure 61 Seats Under Three-Party Merger, Poll Shows
headlines.summary: A new poll suggests a merger between Bennett, Eisenkot, and Liberman's parties could form a 61-seat opposition bloc without relying on Arab parties, challenging Netanyahu's coalition.

headlines.headline: Armenia distances from Russia as Pashinyan says "not an ally" on Ukraine
headlines.summary: Armenian PM Nikol Pashinyan clarified Armenia is not allied with Russia on Ukraine, confirming he will skip Moscow Victory Day parade. The Kremlin summoned Armenia's ambassador after Zelensky's visit to Yerevan, showing tensions as Armenia drifts toward EU.

perspectives.angle: Armenia Distances Itself from Russia as Kremlin Fumes Over Zelensky Visit
perspectives.summary: Armenian PM Pashinyan declared Armenia is not an ally of Russia on Ukraine, while Russia summoned Armenia's ambassador to protest Zelensky's visit to Yerevan, marking a significant shift in Caucasus geopolitics.

headlines.headline: Maersk warns energy crunch to persist even if Iran peace deal struck
headlines.summary: Shipping giant Maersk said the Iran war has pushed fuel costs up nearly $500 million a month, with the energy crisis set to continue even if a peace deal is reached. The warning came as oil supplies tighten further with depleted storage tanks meeting peak summer demand.

perspectives.angle: Maersk Warns Energy Crisis to Persist Even with Iran Peace Deal
perspectives.summary: Shipping giant Maersk reported fuel costs surged nearly $500 million monthly due to the Iran war, warning the energy crisis will continue even if a peace agreement is reached.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Vows No Immunity for Enemies After Beirut Strike
headlines.summary: Israel struck a Hezbollah commander in Beirut, its first strike on the capital since the ceasefire. Netanyahu declared there would be no immunity for militants threatening Israel.

perspectives.angle: Israel Strikes Beirut, Netanyahu Warns No Immunity for Militants
perspectives.summary: Israel carried out its first strike on Beirut's southern suburbs since the ceasefire, targeting a Hezbollah commander. Netanyahu declared there would be "no immunity" for Israel's enemies amid escalating regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Global Views of US Fall Below Russia Under Trump, Survey Finds
headlines.summary: An annual democracy study shows US global perceptions have deteriorated for a second year and now rank worse than Russia, as Trump’s policies strain the NATO alliance.

headlines.headline: Lula Visits Trump Seeking to Avert Trade Tariffs
headlines.summary: Brazilian President Lula met with Trump in Washington to discuss trade relations and tariffs. The two leaders skipped a joint press appearance despite signs of thaw in relations.

headlines.headline: Russia ramps up assassination campaign against opponents abroad, Western intelligence says
headlines.summary: Western intelligence officials report Russia has intensified targeted killings abroad since the 2022 Ukraine invasion, going after Russian activists and foreign supporters of Ukraine using recruited proxies. Plots have been disrupted in France, Germany, Lithuania, and Poland.

perspectives.angle: Russia Ramping Up Assassination Attempts Against Opponents in Europe
perspectives.summary: European intelligence officials warn that Russia is intensifying efforts to kill dissidents and opponents across Europe, marking a dangerous escalation in Moscow's overseas operations.

headlines.headline: US stocks fall from records as oil prices yo-yo amid Iran tensions
headlines.summary: US stocks dropped from their record highs while oil prices fluctuated sharply as tensions between the US and Iran continued to roil global markets.

headlines.headline: Hamas Operating Training Camps in Turkey for Attacks on Israel
headlines.summary: Kan 11 News reports Hamas is using Turkish shooting ranges and drone schools to train operatives for future conflicts with Israel, raising tensions between Israel and Turkey.

headlines.headline: China to Supply J-35 Stealth Jets to Pakistan, Edge Over India
headlines.summary: Chinese J-35 stealth fighter jets are set to give Pakistan an advantage over India in fifth-generation aircraft technology, as India lags in acquiring stealth capabilities.

headlines.headline: UAE Air Defenses Respond to Missile Threat as Regional Tensions Escalate
headlines.summary: UAE authorities activated air defenses in response to a missile threat amid ongoing US-Iran hostilities, urging residents to follow official channels for safety updates.

headlines.headline: Third Round of Lebanon-Israel Direct Talks Set for Next Week in Washington
headlines.summary: Lebanon and Israel will hold their third round of direct negotiations in Washington next week, mediated by the US State Department, continuing unprecedented diplomatic engagement.

headlines.headline: Oil prices jump as US-Iran hostilities threaten ceasefire
headlines.summary: Oil prices rose more than 1% after renewed fighting between the US and Iran threatened a shaky ceasefire and dashed hopes for progress on reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a key transit route.

perspectives.angle: Oil Prices Whipsaw as Iran Conflict Shakes Global Markets
perspectives.summary: Crude oil swung from $88 to $107 per barrel as Iran-UAE attacks and Strait of Hormuz disruptions roiled markets, with WTI settling near $97 amid ongoing US-Iran tensions.

headlines.headline: US-Iran resolution at UN faces Chinese and Russian vetoes
headlines.summary: The US urged countries to support a UN resolution demanding Iran halt attacks on the Strait of Hormuz, but diplomats say China and Russia are likely to veto it, highlighting geopolitical divisions over the conflict.

perspectives.angle: US Iran Resolution at UN Faces Likely Vetoes from China and Russia
perspectives.summary: The US urged countries to support its UN resolution demanding Iran halt attacks and mining of waterways. China and Russia are expected to veto the measure.

headlines.headline: Pussy Riot protests Russia return to Venice Biennale
headlines.summary: Dozens of activists from the anti-Kremlin art collective Pussy Riot demonstrated in Venice, waving Ukrainian flags and chanting against Putin to oppose Moscow presence at the Venice Biennale for the first time since the 2022 invasion.

perspectives.angle: Pussy Riot Protesters Swarm Russia's Venice Biennale Pavilion
perspectives.summary: Activists from Pussy Riot and FEMEN staged a protest against Russia's presence at the Venice Biennale, releasing colored smoke and swarming the pavilion in pink balaclavas.

headlines.headline: Brazil Lula reports progress after Trump White House meeting
headlines.summary: Brazilian President Lula reported progress in relations with the US after a three-hour meeting with Trump in Washington, seeking to avert new trade tariffs that had strained bilateral ties.

perspectives.angle: Brazil's Lula says Trump indicated no plans to invade Cuba during White House meeting
perspectives.summary: Brazilian President Lula da Silva reported that Trump conveyed he has no intention of ordering a US invasion of Cuba during their White House discussion. The statement comes amid heightened US-Cuba tensions.

headlines.headline: Serbia rejects local bid for Russian-owned NIS oil firm
headlines.summary: Serbias President Vucic dismissed a bid by a recently formed local company to purchase a majority stake in the Russian-owned NIS oil firm, citing lack of expertise amid ongoing talks with Hungarian MOL.

headlines.headline: Trump Says Iran Peace Deal Could Be a Week Away
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump said a peace deal with Iran could be reached within a week as Tehran reviews the latest US proposal. The US also paused its "Project Freedom" operations in the Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.angle: U.S. Fires on Iran-Flagged Tankers as Peace Deal Response Awaited
perspectives.summary: U.S. Central Command says forces disabled two Iranian oil tankers attempting to dock at an Iranian port in violation of the American blockade. Secretary of State Rubio said Washington awaits Iran's response to a peace proposal as clashes continue in the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Peace Negotiator Arrives in Miami for Talks with US Envoys
headlines.summary: Ukraine top negotiator Rustem Umerov arrived in Miami for meetings with US representatives as peace talks on ending the war with Russia have stalled in recent months.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Peace Negotiator Arrives in Miami for US Talks
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian peace negotiator Rustem Umerov arrived in Miami for meetings with US officials as peace talks to end the war with Russia have stalled in recent months.

headlines.headline: Dollar Firms as US-Iran Hostilities Flare, Yen Steadies on Intervention Risk
headlines.summary: The dollar strengthened against major currencies after renewed hostilities between the US and Iran, while the Japanese yen held steady following verbal intervention warnings from Tokyo.

headlines.headline: Turkey Unveils First Intercontinental Ballistic Missile Yildirimhan
headlines.summary: Turkey revealed its first ICBM with a 6,000km range and Mach 25 speed, representing a major advancement in Turkish defense capabilities and regional deterrence.

perspectives.angle: Turkey unveils intercontinental ballistic missile program with 6,000 km range
perspectives.summary: Turkey displayed a model of the Yildirimhan ICBM at SAHA 2026 defense expo, a conventionally-armed missile that could reach most of Europe and Asia, marking a significant shift in NATO ally strategic capabilities.

headlines.headline: UK Court Convicts Two Men for Spying on Hong Kong Dissidents
headlines.summary: A UK court found two men guilty, including a British immigration officer, of conducting surveillance on Hong Kong pro-democracy activists in Britain on behalf of China, in an unprecedented shadow policing case.

headlines.headline: German finance minister blames Trump Iran war for economic slowdown
headlines.summary: Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil called Trump's "irresponsible war in Iran" a key factor harming Germany's economy, highlighting growing transatlantic tensions over the conflict.

perspectives.angle: US-Germany Rift Deepens Over Iran War as Trump Orders Troop Withdrawal
perspectives.summary: German Chancellor Merz criticized Trump for having "no strategy" on Iran, prompting the US to announce withdrawal of 5,000 troops from Germany. Berlin dispatched minesweepers to the Mediterranean as the diplomatic row escalates.

headlines.headline: China announces suspended death sentences for former defense ministers
headlines.summary: Two former Chinese defense ministers received suspended death sentences following a sweeping anti-corruption purge that has ousted several top military officials.

headlines.headline: Fears of renewed Gaza war as Hamas disarmament talks stall
headlines.summary: Israeli media reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting amid an impasse in disarmament negotiations with Hamas, raising concerns about a new escalation.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait lift US military access restrictions
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait have lifted restrictions on US military use of their bases and airspace that were imposed after the launch of the US operation to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, according to the Wall Street Journal.

perspectives.angle: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait Lift US Military Access Restrictions
perspectives.summary: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait have lifted restrictions on US military use of their bases and airspace that were imposed after the launch of Operation to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the Wall Street Journal reports.

headlines.headline: Rubio approves .8 billion in weapons sales to Middle East partners
headlines.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio approved the sale of hundreds of air-defense interceptors and other weapons to Middle East partners in deals worth .8 billion as the region remains volatile.

headlines.headline: EU prepares for potential talks with Vladimir Putin
headlines.summary: European Union leaders are preparing for potential talks with Russian President Vladimir Putin, the Financial Times reported, citing a senior official.

headlines.headline: China asks banks to pause loans to US-sanctioned refineries
headlines.summary: China's financial regulator has advised the country's largest lenders to temporarily suspend new loans to five refineries recently sanctioned by the US over their ties to Iranian oil, Bloomberg News reported.

perspectives.angle: China Tells Banks to Halt Loans to US-Sanctioned Iranian Oil Refineries
perspectives.summary: Chinese regulators advised major lenders to suspend new loans to five refineries sanctioned by the US over ties to Iranian oil, signaling Beijing may be bowing to Washington pressure.

headlines.headline: Poland's president pushes referendum on EU climate policy amid political roadblocks
headlines.summary: Polish President Andrzej Duda is advocating for a referendum on EU climate policy, though the proposal faces opposition in the Senate. The debate highlights tensions over energy transition costs and sovereignty.

headlines.headline: China urged to forge 'spear' for cognitive warfare amid great power rivalry
headlines.summary: Prominent Chinese academics are calling on Beijing to move beyond a defensive mindset and actively shape international public opinion. The recommendations come as competition with Western powers intensifies.

headlines.headline: Japan signals unlimited yen defense, in daily contact with US
headlines.summary: Japans top currency diplomat said the country faces no constraints on currency intervention frequency and is in daily contact with US authorities, reinforcing Tokyos resolve to defend the embattled yen.

headlines.headline: India-Pakistan ceasefire holds one year on, but little else does
headlines.summary: One year after the India-Pakistan conflict, the ceasefire continues but underlying tensions remain unresolved. The crisis was triggered by a militant attack on tourists in Kashmir.

headlines.headline: US not planning imminent military action in Cuba despite Trump threats
headlines.summary: US officials say the administration isnt looking at imminent military action against Havana despite Trumps threats, as new sanctions target Cuban military conglomerate.

headlines.headline: British voters cast ballots in elections expected to deal blow to Starmer
headlines.summary: Elections for almost 5,000 council seats in England and to the parliaments in Scotland and Wales could signal the beginning of the end of Britain traditional two-party system if voters opt for populist and nationalist parties.

headlines.headline: Baltic nations seek more NATO air defences after drones hit Latvian oil facility
headlines.summary: Latvia and Lithuania called on NATO to boost air defences after two suspected Ukrainian drones crossed from Russian territory and crashed in Latvia, one exploding at an oil storage facility.

headlines.headline: US imposes sanctions on Cuban military conglomerate and mining joint venture
headlines.summary: The Trump administration targeted sources of foreign investment for Cuba communist leaders by sanctioning a business conglomerate run by Cubas military and a Cuban-Canadian mining joint venture.

headlines.headline: Xi Positions for Stability as Iran War Casts Shadow Over Trump Summit
headlines.summary: As Xi Jinping prepares to host Trump, Beijing faces the challenge of managing China-Iran economic ties while seeking to preserve the US-China relationship. The Iran conflict threatens to dominate the Beijing summit agenda.

headlines.headline: Iran War Threatens Global Food Security, UN Warns
headlines.summary: The Hormuz blockade is cutting off vital agricultural inputs, with the UN FAO warning of harvest risks. China identified as key to maintaining food supply chains amid the crisis.

headlines.headline: CIA Assessment: Iran Retains 70% of Pre-War Missile Stockpile, Can Withstand Blockade for Months
headlines.summary: Intelligence report contradicts public statements from Trump officials, suggesting Iran has significant military capacity remaining and can endure the Strait of Hormuz blockade.

perspectives.angle: US Intelligence Estimates Iran Can Outlast Hormuz Blockade for Months
perspectives.summary: Iran retains about 75% of prewar inventories of mobile launchers and 70% of missile stockpiles, according to a Washington Post report. The assessment suggests Tehran has significant resilience despite ongoing conflict.
